review_notes: The functions are mostly accurate and map well to Kubernetes RBAC controls, which are relevant for enforcing workforce security as per HIPAA requirements. However, the function 'k8s_rbac_role_binding_review' was too vague and has been renamed to better reflect its purpose of ensuring least privilege in role bindings.
naming_improvements: Ensure function names clearly describe the specific security check being performed, such as 'least_privilege' in role bindings.
title: 164_308_a_3_i: 164.308(a)(3)(i) Workforce security
step1_reasoning: The HIPAA control 164.308(a)(3)(i) focuses on workforce security, which involves managing access controls to ensure that only authorized personnel have access to sensitive data. In Kubernetes, this translates to implementing robust RBAC policies to enforce least privilege, ensuring that no users or service accounts have unnecessary cluster-admin privileges, and reviewing role bindings to prevent unauthorized access. Additionally, disabling service account token automounting by default helps mitigate risks of token misuse.
